F.plain: Best-τ F1 of the ACDC-discovered inter-cell edge set against the exhaustive-oracle true data-flow graph.
F.note: acdc.jl:718-723 (F = best_score.f1); true graph = exhaustive do-set 0/base+17/base+37 over the candidate cells. This is exactly the paper's circuit-discovery F = edge F1 vs the true data-flow graph. Record: extra.triad.F and top-level value (e.g. acdc_pong.json F=0.154; battery mean F=0.625, n=35).

S.formula: S = scrubbing_preserved(discovered_circuit) = fraction of clean candidate-cell readouts unchanged when non-circuit parents are resample-scrubbed
S.plain: Fraction of the clean readouts the discovered circuit alone reproduces when everything outside it is resample-ablated (bit-exact re-run).
S.note: acdc.jl (S = scrub_preserved). A held-out-flavoured sufficiency on bit-exact re-runs; the new §3 (sec:triad) admits the fraction-within-tolerance [0,1] special case of the held-out predictive test, and this preservation fraction is that form, so it satisfies §3. Record: extra.triad.S (acdc_pong.json S=0.625).

M.note: acdc.jl now emits M = |true edges|/|discovered edges|, the paper's M = |U*|/|U_hat| at the edge level (as path_patching does). Record: extra.triad.M with M_note '|U*|=12 true edges / |U_hat|=1 discovered edges (best-F1 tau)' (acdc_pong.json M=1.0). Replaced the former edge-precision (1 - FP/n_disc).

F.formula: F = 1.0 by construction (recovered == exact); leaderboard passes clamp(1 − |max_abs_recovered−exact|); position record uses F = ρ(recovered_px, exact_px)
F.plain: The patch literally re-runs the exact single-site intervention, so recovered effect equals the oracle effect and the causal-effect agreement is 1.
F.note: activation_patching.jl:582-597 main record (metric max_abs_recovered_minus_exact=0 -> F=1.0); jl:693 position record F = _pearson(rec_px, ex_px). Both are the paper's Phase-C patching F = causal-effect-agreement correlation vs Delta_y(u); here exact by construction. Record: extra.triad.F=1.0 (battery mean F=1.0, n=84).

source.desc: main record: max_abs_recovered_minus_exact = 0 ⇒ F = 1

S.formula: S = fraction of held-out do(u) whose predicted Δy (least-squares a·attr+b fit on a disjoint calibration half) lands within ε of the bit-exact re-run
S.plain: Held-out predictive sufficiency: fit the attribution->Δy map on a calibration half of the causes, predict the disjoint held-out half, count hits within an ε band.
S.note: common/triad_sm.jl:111-155 sufficiency_score, called via triad_extra_dict (activation_patching.jl:597). Exactly the paper's held-out predictive estimator on the oracle's per-cause bit-exact re-runs. Record: extra.triad.S (activation_patching_pong.json S=1.0; battery mean S=0.996, n=84).

F.formula: F = scrubbing_preserved_performance_true = fraction of the true-graph sink readouts preserved when every candidate OUTSIDE the true hypothesised circuit is resample-ablated
F.formula_tex: F = \dfrac{\#\{\text{true-circuit sink readouts preserved under scrub}\}}{\#\ \text{readouts}} \in [0,1]
F.plain: For the ground-truth hypothesised circuit, the fraction of the behaviour (true-graph sink cells) preserved across the resample distribution.
F.note: causal_scrubbing.jl:650-670 (F = clamp(true_preserved)). The hypothesis is built FROM the A1 oracle data-flow (ancestors of the output), so it is oracle-grounded, but F is a scrubbing-preservation fraction, NOT the paper's edge-F1 (circuit discovery) nor a causal-effect-agreement correlation (patching/mediation). It measures 'does the oracle-derived circuit pass the scrub' rather than 'how well does the method's discovered claim agree with the oracle'. Record: extra.triad.F / top-level value (pong F=1.0; battery mean F=0.979, n=42).

source.desc: true_preserved: fraction of true-circuit readouts kept under resample-scrub
source.label: causal_scrubbing.jl:547

source.desc: F = clamp(true_preserved)
source.label: causal_scrubbing.jl:699

S.formula: S = true_preserved (same scalar as F): held-out scrubbing sufficiency = fraction of readouts preserved over held-out resample trials
S.plain: Held-out scrubbing sufficiency; equals the F scalar here.
S.note: causal_scrubbing.jl (S = clamp(true_preserved)). A bit-exact-re-run preservation fraction in [0,1]; the new §3 (sec:triad) admits the fraction-within-tolerance [0,1] special case of the held-out test, which this satisfies. Record: extra.triad.S (pong S=1.0).

F.formula: F = interchange_accuracy_aligned = fraction of aligned variables whose interchange (swap SOURCE->BASE, re-run) reproduces the high-level abstraction o_V := source(V) at the readout  (= 1.0 by construction)
F.plain: Interchange accuracy of the SUPPLIED alignment: for each aligned cell, does swapping its value reproduce the predicted high-level outcome; 1.0 by construction on the VCS.
F.note: das.jl:638 (F = clamp(iia_aligned)); metric_name interchange_accuracy_aligned. It IS a causal check (each interchange is a real do(cell:=source) bit-exact re-run, so single-cell interchange == the exact patch oracle), but F verifies a SUPPLIED/known-true alignment reproduces the abstraction (Geiger IIA) rather than scoring a method-discovered claim's agreement against {Delta_y(u)} -> '1.0 by construction'. The genuine discovery signal (alignment_accuracy, whether DAS recovers the right cell) is a separate extra field, not F. Record: extra.triad.F=1.0 (battery mean F=1.0, n=42).

source.desc: F = clamp(interchange accuracy) into the triad; each interchange is a do(cell:=source) re-run
source.label: das.jl:638

F.formula: F = point-biserial rho(per-cell selectivity, oracle causally-used indicator)  over probeable cells
F.plain: Point-biserial correlation between per-cell probe selectivity and the oracle causally-used indicator: does selectivity track causal use (not mere presence)?
F.note: linear_probing.jl now emits top-level metric_name=probe_selectivity_vs_causal_use_correlation with extra.triad.F = point-biserial rho(selectivity, oracle causally-used indicator); it replaced the mean control-task selectivity (a decodability/presence stat). This is §3's correlation between the attribution and the true causal effect. Record: linear_probing_pong.json extra.triad.F=-0.090 (present-but-unused => low/negative).

This is §3's causal-agreement F for a readout-direction method. Record: logit_lens_pong.json extra.triad.F=0.438.

Record: extra.triad.M, M_true_minimal_size/M_named_size (battery mean M=1.0 over the n=18 games with >=1 discovered edge).

Record: sae_pong.json extra.triad.F=0.041. (The top-level 'value' field is still the feature_variable_matched_fraction naming stat, but the committed triad F is the correct causal Spearman.)
